generations.code:
Let me think about the set of players that X cannot beat directly. Call this set D(X) = {Y : a_Y > a_X AND b_Y > b_X}.

For X to win, all players in D(X) must be eliminated by other players. This is possible as long as D(X) can be cleared. 

The player with the highest a among all players - if they also have the highest b, they dominate everyone and only they can win. Otherwise...

Actually the key insight from competitive programming: Player X can win if and only if the set of players that dominate X (beat X on both maps) can all be eliminated. A player in D(X) can be beaten by someone outside D(X) who beats them on at least one map.

The simplest characterization: X can win if and only if there is no player that dominates X AND cannot be beaten by anyone else before facing X. 

Actually I think the answer is: X can win if and only if the player with the maximum a_i value OR the player with maximum b_i value can be "handled". 

Let me reconsider: The player who has max(a) - call them A_max. A_max can beat everyone on map 1. So A_max can always win. Similarly for B_max.

For any other player X: X cannot beat A_max on map 1. X can beat A_max only on map 2 if b_X > b_{A_max}. If X can't beat A_max on map 2 either, then A_max dominates X. But A_max can be beaten by B_max (if B_max ≠ A_max) on map 2... So X can win if B_max can eliminate A_max.

I think: X can win if and only if X can beat every player that X must face, where the game master can arrange for "dangerous" players to be eliminated by others first. 

The condition simplifies to: X can win iff for every player Y that dominates X, Y can be beaten by some player Z (on some map), and Z can be beaten by X or arranged to lose before the final.

After more research/thinking: X can win iff X is not dominated by any player, OR all players dominating X are themselves dominated by someone X can beat.

Let me just implement: X can win iff there's no player Y such that Y dominates X AND Y is not dominated by any player that X can beat.

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        can_win = True
        for j in range(n):
            if a[j] > a[i] and b[j] > b[i]:
                # j dominates i, check if someone can beat j that i can beat or arrange
                # j must be beaten by someone; that someone must be beatable by i or arrangeable
                dominated = True
                for k in range(n):
                    if k != j and (a[k] > a[j] or b[k] > b[j]):
                        if a[i] > a[k] or b[i] > b[k]:
                            dominated = False
                            break
                if dominated:
                    can_win = False
                    break
        res.append('1' if can_win else '0')
    print(''.join(res))

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        can_win = True
        for j in range(n):
            if a[j] > a[i] and b[j] > b[i]:
                # j dominates i, check if someone can beat j that i can beat or arrange
                # j must be beaten by someone; that someone must be beatable by i or arrangeable
                dominated = True
                for k in range(n):
                    if k != j and (a[k] > a[j] or b[k] > b[j]):
                        if a[i] > a[k] or b[i] > b[k]:
                            dominated = False
                            break
                if dominated:
                    can_win = False
                    break
        res.append('1' if can_win else '0')
    print(''.join(res))
